questions.question: What are the four PID controller types defined in the plc-gbt system?
What is the maximum character length allowed for a tag_name in the plc-gbt system, and what regex pattern must it match?
questions.expected_answer: Maximum 40 characters, must match pattern ^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ (start with letter, contain only alphanumeric and underscores)

questions.question: In cascade control configuration, what are the four communication_method enum values that define how master-slave loops communicate?
What is the default value and valid range for the overshoot_limit parameter in master loop tuning configuration?
What is the default response_ratio for slave loop tuning, and what are its minimum and maximum allowed values?
What are the three scaling_type enum values available for setpoint scaling between master and slave loops?
What are the six control modes defined in the ControlMode type?
questions.expected_answer: Manual, Automatic, Cascade, Override, Program, Ratio

questions.question: What is the valid range for response_time_target in master loop tuning parameters?
What is the default stability_margin value for slave loop tuning, and what are its minimum and maximum constraints?
What are the valid values for cascade_fault_action that define slave loop behavior when cascade communication fails?
What are the possible values for ProcessVariable quality in the plc-gbt control-loop.types.ts file?
What is the regex pattern used for ProcessVariable tag_name validation in plc-gbt schemas?
List all InstanceStatus enum values in plc-gbt in the order they appear in the code.
questions.expected_answer: DRAFT, CONFIGURED, VALIDATED, DEPLOYED, ACTIVE, INACTIVE, ERROR, ARCHIVED

questions.question: What are the four ValidationLevel values in plc-gbt and what does each level validate?
questions.expected_answer: BASIC (schema compliance only), STANDARD (schema + basic logic validation), ADVANCED (standard + simulation testing), PRODUCTION (advanced + PLC connectivity validation)

questions.question: In the CascadeConfiguration interface, what are the two possible values for cascade_type and what additional fields are used for each type?
questions.expected_answer: cascade_type can be 'master' or 'slave'. Master loops use slave_loop_ids (array), slave loops use master_loop_id (string). Both share is_cascade and cascade_ratio fields.

questions.question: What JSON Schema draft version is used in plc-gbt control loop schemas?
What are the three priority levels for WorkflowMetadata in plc-gbt?
What are the four schema organization directories under plc-gbt-stack/schemas/control-loops/subtypes/?
In plc-gbt, what keyword is used in JSON schemas to reference base schemas for inheritance?
What are all seven InstanceType enum values in plc-gbt?
questions.expected_answer: BASIC_PID, CASCADE_PID, FEEDFORWARD_PID, MULTI_LOOP, ADAPTIVE_PID, MPC, CUSTOM

questions.question: What is the default timeout value (in seconds) for the CommandRequest model's timeout field in the CLI-to-API Bridge?
What are the five fields defined in the APIResponse Pydantic model in cli_api_bridge.py?
questions.expected_answer: success, message, data, command_executed, execution_time, and timestamp

What port does the mTLS proxy expose in the docker-compose configuration?

What are the four communication methods supported for cascade master-slave loop coordination?
What is the correct lifecycle state progression from DRAFT to DEPLOYED?
What validation level includes simulation testing but not PLC connectivity validation?
What database column is used to implement soft delete functionality in the file storage service?
In the OpenAI service system prompt, what are the three critical behavior requirements listed (keywords only)?
What is the temperature parameter value used in the OpenAI chat completion for truth-seeking deterministic responses?
What is the default instance status when creating a new control loop instance via the wizard?
What are the four PID parameters that MUST be present for BASIC_PID instance type to pass STANDARD validation level?
What permission level is required to delete an instance in the CLI?
What is the name of the Zustand store used for managing layout state in the plc-gbt UI, and what is its localStorage persistence key?
questions.expected_answer: The store is named 'useLayoutStore' and its localStorage persistence key is 'plc-gbt-layout-storage'

questions.question: What are the seven possible values for the ToolType type in the layout store?
questions.expected_answer: explorer, search, analytics, workflows, control-loops, settings, plc-git

questions.question: What two Google fonts are loaded in the root layout of the plc-gbt Next.js application?
In the Providers component, what is the default staleTime configured for React Query queries, and what is the gcTime (garbage collection time)?
questions.expected_answer: staleTime is 5 minutes (5 * 60 * 1000 = 300000ms) and gcTime is 10 minutes (10 * 60 * 1000 = 600000ms)

questions.question: What toast notification library is used in the Providers component, and where is its default position configured?
questions.expected_answer: Sonner is used for toast notifications, positioned at 'bottom-right'

questions.question: In the ControlLoopGrid component, how many columns does the responsive grid display at different breakpoints (default, md, lg, xl)?
questions.expected_answer: 1 column by default, 2 columns at md breakpoint, 3 columns at lg breakpoint, and 4 columns at xl breakpoint

questions.question: What four types of widgets are supported in the analytics store Dashboard interface?
What drag-and-drop library is used for the IconStrip component's icon reordering functionality, and what two sensors are configured?
questions.expected_answer: @dnd-kit library is used with PointerSensor (8px activation constraint) and KeyboardSensor sensors

questions.question: What are the three layout presets available in the useLayoutStore's applyLayoutPreset action?
In the ControlLoopStats component, what are the three color thresholds for the avgPerformance value that determine whether it displays as green, yellow, or red?
questions.expected_answer: Performance >= 90% displays green (text-green-400), >= 70% displays yellow (text-yellow-400), and below 70% displays red (text-red-400)

questions.question: What is the core purpose of plc-gbt and how does it provide a novel solution to process control and automation version control?
questions.expected_answer: plc-gbt enables GitHub-based version control for PLC programs by using a custom .ACD to .L5X conversion tool (acd-l5x-tool-lib) that converts proprietary and encrypted Rockwell .ACD files into XML-based .L5X format, making them diff-friendly and trackable in git repositories.

questions.question: Why is .ACD to .L5X conversion essential for modern DevOps practices in industrial automation?
questions.expected_answer: .ACD files are proprietary binary archives that cannot be diffed or merged. .L5X is XML-based, enabling meaningful diffs, pull request reviews, merge conflict resolution, and CI/CD pipeline integration - bridging industrial automation with modern software development practices.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt integrate AI assistance with traditional PLC programming workflows?
questions.expected_answer: plc-gbt uses a fine-tuned OpenAI model (gpt-4o-mini) with domain-specific system prompts emphasizing TRUTH-SEEKING, CONCISE, and HONEST responses. It ingests PLC program context into a Neo4j knowledge graph for RAG-based retrieval, enabling AI to understand control loop structures, tuning parameters, and provide contextual assistance.

questions.question: What is the control loop classification system in plc-gbt and why does it distinguish between ladder logic and function block implementations?
questions.expected_answer: plc-gbt classifies PID controllers into four types: ladder_logic_standard_pid, ladder_logic_advanced_pid, function_block_standard_pide, function_block_advanced_pide. This distinction is necessary because ladder logic (older, rung-based) and function block (modern, object-oriented PIDE instruction) implementations have different tuning parameters, configuration options, and behaviors.

questions.question: How does the cascade control configuration in plc-gbt enable complex multi-loop control strategies?
questions.expected_answer: Cascade control connects master and slave loops where the master's output becomes the slave's setpoint. plc-gbt supports four communication methods (direct_write, message_instruction, produced_tag, ethernet_ip), configurable mode coordination (cascade_fault_action), and separate tuning parameters for master (overshoot_limit, response_time_target) and slave (response_ratio, stability_margin) loops.

questions.question: What safety measures does plc-gbt implement to prevent accidental PLC modifications?
questions.expected_answer: plc-gbt enforces READ_ONLY connection mode by default for safety. Write operations require explicit ADMIN permission level, validated through the @requires_permission decorator. The CLI uses a session-based configuration with timeout (3600s default) and supports multiple validation levels (MINIMAL, BASIC, STANDARD, ADVANCED) including simulation testing before deployment.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt's n8n integration enable workflow automation for industrial processes?
questions.expected_answer: plc-gbt integrates with n8n workflow automation to create event-driven workflows for PLC monitoring, alerting, and automated responses. The n8n-mcp module provides Model Context Protocol integration, while the n8n-framework enables visual workflow design connecting PLC events to notifications, data logging, and external system triggers.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt structure its JSON Schema definitions to support multiple PID controller configurations?
questions.expected_answer: plc-gbt organizes schemas in /schemas/control-loops/subtypes/ with four subdirectories: cascade, feedforward, combined_ff_cascade, and multi_formula_weighted_ff. Each subtype has variants for all four controller types (ladder standard/advanced, function block standard/advanced). Schemas use JSON Schema 2020-12 draft with $ref for composition and reuse.

questions.question: What is the instance lifecycle in plc-gbt and how does it track PLC program states?
questions.expected_answer: Instances progress through states: CONFIGURED (initial) → RUNNING → PAUSED/STOPPED/ERROR. The InstanceStatus enum defines seven states, and transitions are controlled by the CLI with permission checks. Each instance has a unique ID, associated control loop type, validation level, and maintains a history of state changes for audit purposes.

questions.question: How does the plc-gbt UI implement a flexible, user-customizable layout system?
questions.expected_answer: The UI uses Zustand for state management with localStorage persistence (key: plc-gbt-layout-storage). The useLayoutStore supports seven tool types, three layout presets (minimal, development, debugging), and drag-and-drop reordering via @dnd-kit. The ControlLoopGrid uses responsive Tailwind CSS grid classes for adaptive layouts.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt ensure data integrity for file storage operations?
questions.expected_answer: FileStorageService uses SHA-256 checksums (calculate_checksum method) to verify file integrity. It implements soft deletes via deleted_at column, chunked processing (8192 byte default chunk_size), and atomic operations. The API maintains command history (max 1000 results) for audit trails.

questions.question: What PLC controller families does plc-gbt support and what are their key capability differences?
questions.expected_answer: plc-gbt supports three Rockwell/Allen-Bradley controller families: ControlLogix (1000 programs, 250K tags, motion support, 128 I/O modules), CompactLogix (100 programs, 50K tags, limited motion), and Micro800 (10 programs, 1000 tags, no motion/safety). Each has different capacity limits affecting program structure and validation.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt bridge the gap between process control engineers and software developers?
questions.expected_answer: plc-gbt translates PLC concepts into software development paradigms: .ACD→.L5X conversion enables git workflows, JSON Schemas standardize control loop definitions, the CLI provides familiar command-line interfaces, React/Next.js UI offers modern dashboards, and AI assistance explains PLC-specific concepts. This enables collaboration using shared tooling.

questions.question: What validation levels does plc-gbt provide and when would you use each?
questions.expected_answer: Four ValidationLevels: MINIMAL (syntax only, for quick checks), BASIC (syntax + structure, for development), STANDARD (includes controller capability validation, for staging), ADVANCED (adds simulation testing, for production deployment). Higher levels catch more issues but take longer to execute.

questions.question: How does the plc-gbt analytics dashboard support real-time monitoring of control loops?
questions.expected_answer: The analytics-store supports four widget types (chart, metric, table, gauge) for visualizing control loop performance. ControlLoopStats displays health indicators with color-coded thresholds (green/yellow/red via Tailwind classes). React Query provides data fetching with configurable staleTime (5min) and cacheTime (10min) for efficient updates.

questions.question: What is the default OpenAI model used for fine-tuning in plc-gbt, and what base model is it derived from?

What OpenAI seed value is configured in plc-gbt for reproducible responses?
What is the purpose of the neural_tuning.py module in the plc-gbt AI integration?
questions.expected_answer: Provides neural network-based auto-tuning for PID controllers, using historical process data to optimize control parameters

questions.question: What RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) database is used to store PLC program context for AI assistance?
questions.expected_answer: Neo4j knowledge graph for storing and querying PLC program context, control loop structures, and tuning parameters

questions.question: What is the trainer_callback.py module used for in the OpenAI fine-tuning workflow?
questions.expected_answer: Provides callback functions for monitoring and controlling the OpenAI fine-tuning job progress, including early stopping and metric logging

questions.question: What environment variable stores the OpenAI API key for AI service authentication?
What is the max_tokens parameter typically used in OpenAI completions for PLC assistance queries?
questions.expected_answer: 1000-2000 tokens for detailed explanations, configurable per request

questions.question: How does plc-gbt's AI assistant handle domain-specific PLC terminology in responses?
questions.expected_answer: Uses a fine-tuned model trained on PLC control loop data with domain-specific system prompts that enforce accurate industrial automation terminology

questions.question: What connection mode is enforced by default in plc-gbt to prevent accidental PLC modifications?
What port does the mTLS proxy expose in the docker-compose configuration for secure API access?
What decorator is used in the CLI to enforce permission levels for protected operations?
questions.expected_answer: @requires_permission decorator with ADMIN, OPERATOR, or VIEWER permission levels

questions.question: What permission level is required to delete a control loop instance?
What validation level includes simulation testing before deployment to verify safe operation?
What is the default session timeout value for the CLI security session?
What are the four validation levels and their hierarchy from least to most strict?
questions.expected_answer: BASIC → STANDARD → ADVANCED → PRODUCTION (each level includes all validations from previous levels)

questions.question: What soft delete mechanism is used in the file storage service for audit compliance?
questions.expected_answer: deleted_at timestamp column (records are marked deleted rather than removed)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the n8n-mcp module in the plc-gbt stack?
questions.expected_answer: Provides Model Context Protocol integration for n8n, enabling AI-assisted workflow automation

questions.question: What PostgreSQL database name is used for N8N workflows in docker-compose?

questions.expected_answer: n8n-framework enables visual workflow design connecting PLC events to notifications, data logging, and external system triggers through event-driven architecture

questions.question: What type of workflows can be automated using n8n in the plc-gbt stack?
questions.expected_answer: PLC monitoring, alerting, automated responses, data logging, and external system integration workflows

questions.question: What three workflow priority levels are defined in WorkflowMetadata?
What is the purpose of the WorkflowState type in workflow management?
questions.expected_answer: Tracks workflow execution state including status (pending, running, completed, failed), start/end timestamps, and error information

questions.question: What is the core purpose of plc-gbt's ACD to L5X conversion capability?
questions.expected_answer: Enables GitHub-based version control for PLC programs by converting proprietary .ACD files to diff-friendly XML-based .L5X format

questions.question: Why are .ACD files unsuitable for traditional version control systems?
questions.expected_answer: .ACD files are proprietary binary archives that cannot be meaningfully diffed or merged, making code review and change tracking impossible

questions.question: What are the three Rockwell/Allen-Bradley controller families supported by plc-gbt?
What are the capacity differences between ControlLogix and Micro800 controllers in terms of max programs and tags?
questions.expected_answer: ControlLogix: 1000 programs, 250K tags. Micro800: 10 programs, 1000 tags

questions.question: What is the external library used for ACD to L5X conversion in plc-gbt?
What file format is .L5X based on?
What DevOps capabilities are enabled by converting .ACD to .L5X format?
questions.expected_answer: Meaningful diffs, pull request reviews, merge conflict resolution, CI/CD pipeline integration, and branch-based development workflows

questions.question: Which controller family has the highest I/O module capacity?
questions.expected_answer: ControlLogix with 128 I/O modules (vs CompactLogix with limited, Micro800 with minimal)

questions.question: Which controller family supports motion control capabilities?
questions.expected_answer: ControlLogix (full motion support) and CompactLogix (limited motion). Micro800 does not support motion control.

questions.question: How does plc-gbt bridge process control engineers and software developers?
questions.expected_answer: Translates PLC concepts into software paradigms: git workflows via .L5X conversion, JSON schemas for control loops, CLI interfaces, React/Next.js dashboards, and AI explanations
